description_long: This is a rectangular stone obelisk with a weathered, classical appearance, likely made from marble or limestone. The front face features a large, inscribed panel with Latin text, partially eroded and chipped at the lower right corner. The top of the obelisk is adorned with a sculpted, scroll-like decorative motif, giving it a distinct ornamental flourish. The sides and back are plain, with the stone showing signs of age and wear, including chips and surface irregularities. The base is robust and slightly stepped, providing stability and a sense of monumentality. The overall coloration is a pale, off-white to light beige, consistent with aged stone.
description: A rectangular, inscribed stone obelisk with a decorative scroll top and a weathered, classical appearance. The front features Latin inscriptions, while the sides and back are plain and aged.
description_view_0: Front view showing the inscribed panel with Latin text and the ornate scroll-like top; lower right corner is chipped.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the plain, unadorned surface and the thickness of the stone; no inscriptions visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the underside of the scroll top and the plain back surface; some interior shadowing visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with a plain surface and visible base structure.
attribute_inscription: The front face contains a large, rectangular panel of Latin inscriptions, carved in a classical serif style. The text is partially eroded and chipped, especially at the lower right corner, but remains mostly legible.
attribute_ornamentation: The top of the obelisk features a sculpted, scroll-like decorative motif, adding an ornamental flourish to the otherwise plain structure.
attribute_base_design: The base is rectangular and robust, with a slightly stepped design and simple linear moldings. It is made of the same stone as the rest of the obelisk and shows signs of chipping and wear.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is not pointed but instead capped with a scroll-like, carved decorative element, giving it a rounded and ornate finish.
